name: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England
tagline: The Coast Guard guarding the rugged coast of Maine and northern New England, based in South Portland.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (South Portland, ME). CONUS full template (BAH MHA ME139 Portland; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Oversees the Maine/New Hampshire coast and waters; SAR + law enforcement. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network/local. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Maine is TRICARE East. Cold winters; scenic coast. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 04106 (South Portland).

data.Role: Coast Guard hub for Maine and northern New England
data.Mission: Search and rescue and law enforcement along the rugged northern New England coast
data.Setting: Scenic coastal Maine (four real seasons, cold winters)

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (official)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England oversees the waters off Maine and New Hampshire, coordinating search and rescue and law enforcement along a rugged, often cold and foggy coast. It's headquartered in South Portland on Casco Bay.
It's in the greater Portland, Maine area, a scenic, increasingly popular coastal region with a strong quality of life, four real seasons, and cold winters.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (official)

sections.title: Coastal Maine Living: Scenic and Increasingly Popular

data.subtitle: Greater Portland is a desirable, growing coastal area, which has lifted housing costs. The main choice is which nearby town fits your commute and schools.
intro: Portland's popularity has pushed up prices in recent years, but the broader area still offers options. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent or buy nearby.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across South Portland, Scarborough, and the surrounding towns, with winters and the coast as part of the picture.

recommendations.advice: South Portland and Cape Elizabeth keep the commute short and put you near the water.
Scarborough, Falmouth, and Gorham are popular with families for schools.
Towns a bit farther from Portland trade a longer commute for lower prices.
Real New England winters; budget for heating and winter driving, and enjoy the coast in summer.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (official)

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (official)

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in the area is limited, so most families rent or buy across greater Portland.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: Coast Guard Sector Northern New England (official)
DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA ME139 (Portland) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. South Portland, Scarborough, Cape Elizabeth, and Falmouth districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Sector Northern New England
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and greater Portland hospitals

data.Commissary: Limited locally; most groceries via greater Portland stores

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and greater Portland's popularity has raised prices, so tour length matters.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
Portland prices have risen and you want flexibility.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour and want to settle on the coast.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
A town fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a community and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Greater Portland has gotten pricier as it's grown, so weigh your tour length; towns farther out offer more value. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on South Portland, Scarborough, and the area before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Maine vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (South Portland, Scarborough, Cape Elizabeth, or Falmouth) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; the base clinic serves the assigned population.

data.q: What is Sector Northern New England?
data.a: It's the Coast Guard's hub for Maine and northern New England, coordinating search and rescue and law enforcement along the coast, based in South Portland.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. Portland is MHA ME139.
Across greater Portland. South Portland and Cape Elizabeth are closest; Scarborough, Falmouth, and Gorham are popular for schools. CG housing is limited.
Four real seasons with cold, snowy winters and beautiful summers on the coast. Budget for heating and winter driving.
